games.title: OXO
games.summary: Early graphical computer game and human-computer interaction demonstration.

games.title: Tennis for Two
games.summary: Public exhibit designed for visitors at Brookhaven; not a raster-TV product.

games.title: Spacewar!
games.summary: Foundational real-time competitive computer game and shared program culture.

games.title: Brown Box / Magnavox Odyssey
games.summary: Prototype-to-product home television game apparatus associated with Baer, Rusch and Harrison.

games.title: Computer Space
games.summary: Nutting Associates arcade game commercially derived from ideas demonstrated by Spacewar!, created by Nolan Bushnell and Ted Dabney.

games.title: Pong
games.summary: Readable arcade hit that helped demonstrate pay-per-play commercial viability.

games.title: Positron
games.summary: Gary Partis profile lists Positron among early BBC/Electron works.

games.title: Doctor Who and the Mines of Terror
games.summary: Micro Power title credited to Gary Partis on BBC Micro archive records.

games.title: Psycastria
games.summary: Gary Partis profile describes the game as a major 1986 release and part of his contemporary recognition.

games.title: Syncron
games.summary: A&B profile discusses Syncron and Superior publication context.

games.title: Winter Olympiad
games.summary: Kevin Blake portfolio names the title among co-authored Tynesoft sports work.

games.title: Ian Botham's Test Match
games.summary: Kevin Blake portfolio uses this title to discuss sales, ports and returns.

games.title: Spy vs Spy
games.summary: Kevin Blake portfolio describes conversion work and manual reconstruction from another version.

games.title: Ravenskull
games.summary: Early Reflections/Superior title lead; needs stronger contemporary source before detailed claims.

games.title: Codename: DROID
games.summary: Early Reflections/Superior title lead used for the technical innovation section.

games.title: Test Drive 4
games.summary: Later Pitbull/Midway line title; epilogue only.
